name: TLBX
description: 2HP precision toolbox: a 1-to-2 buffered mult, a 2-to-1 mix/adder, and a 2-to-2 adder/subtractor. Sections cascade through normalization, turning the module into a mult, mixer, 2x/1.5x/3x/6x voltage multiplier, octave transposer, range converter or comparator.

inputs.name: MULT IN
inputs.description: Input to the 1-to-2 precision multiple. When unpatched, OUT1 and OUT2 become 1V and 5V references.

inputs.name: MIX IN1
inputs.description: First mixer input. When left unpatched, MIX OUT copies MULT IN (input normalization from MULT).

inputs.name: MIX IN2
inputs.description: Second mixer input. Normalized to IN1 when unpatched, so a signal on IN1 alone appears doubled at MIX OUT (precision 2x).

inputs.name: DIFF A
inputs.description: First DIFF input. When both DIFF inputs are unpatched, DIFF takes its signal from MIX OUT (input normalization from MIX).

inputs.name: DIFF B
inputs.description: Second DIFF input. If only DIFF A is patched, A+B copies A and A-B copies A. If only DIFF B is patched, A+B copies B and A-B outputs an inverted copy of B.

outputs.name: MULT OUT1 (1V)
outputs.description: Buffered copy of MULT IN. When MULT IN is unpatched, outputs a 1V reference (typ. <±0.5mV, max <±1mV). If left unpatched while MULT IN is patched, the mult signal is also summed into MIX OUT (output normalization).

outputs.name: MULT OUT2 (5V)
outputs.description: Buffered copy of MULT IN. When MULT IN is unpatched, outputs a 5V reference (typ. <±1mV, max <±2.5mV). If left unpatched while MULT IN is patched, the mult signal is summed into both DIFF outputs (output normalization).

outputs.name: MIX OUT
outputs.description: Precision sum of MIX IN1 + MIX IN2. If MIX OUT is unpatched, the mix is passed to the DIFF section at full gain into A+B and halved into A-B (output normalization).

outputs.name: DIFF A+B
outputs.description: Precision sum of DIFF A and DIFF B.

outputs.name: DIFF A-B
outputs.description: Precision difference (A minus B). Combined with A+B gives a 2-to-2 adder/subtractor; can also act as an inverter when only B is patched.

behaviors.name: 1V / 5V voltage references
behaviors.description: MULT OUT1 outputs 1V (<±1mV typ), MULT OUT2 outputs 5V (<±2.5mV typ). Used for tuning VCOs, calibrating attenuverters, driving quantizers to a known octave, or feeding the rest of the module as a DC source. The reference path is excluded from the inter-section normalization.

behaviors.name: Section normalization cascade
behaviors.trigger: leave a section's output unpatched while its inputs are driven
behaviors.description: MULT OUT1 unpatched -> mult signal sums into MIX OUT. MULT OUT2 unpatched -> mult signal sums into both DIFF outs. MIX OUT unpatched -> mix feeds DIFF A+B at x1 and A-B at x0.5. Breaking a cable into a downstream input breaks that section's input normalization.

behaviors.name: Precision 2x multiplier (MIX)
behaviors.trigger: patch a signal into MIX IN1 only
behaviors.description: With MIX IN2 unpatched, IN2 is normalized to IN1 and MIX OUT = 2 x IN1. Combined with the MULT-to-MIX cascade, a single input into MULT IN can reach 3x or 6x at the A+B output.

behaviors.name: Adder / subtractor / inverter (DIFF)
behaviors.description: Both inputs -> A+B is the sum, A-B is the difference. Only A patched -> both outs copy A (doubles as a 1-to-2 mult). Only B patched -> A+B copies B, A-B outputs -B, giving a clean signal inverter.

behaviors.name: Octave transposer
behaviors.trigger: patch MULT OUT1 (1V ref) into a DIFF input and run V/Oct through the other
behaviors.description: Adding or subtracting the calibrated 1V reference transposes a 1V/Oct pitch line by exactly one octave. Using MULT OUT2 (5V) the same way gives 5-octave shifts; doubling via MIX yields 2-octave shifts.

behaviors.name: Voltage range conversion
behaviors.trigger: combine MIX doubling with a DIFF subtraction of 5V
behaviors.description: 0-5V -> -5..+5V by doubling then subtracting 5V. -5..+5V -> 0-5V by adding 5V and halving. TLBX replaces a pile of offset and scale utilities for bipolar/unipolar conversions.

behaviors.name: Comparator via feedback
behaviors.trigger: patch MIX OUT back into MIX IN2 (or similar self-feedback)
behaviors.description: DC-coupled, high-gain feedback through the mix path saturates to the op-amp rails, producing roughly +/-10V based on whether the input is above or below 0V. Good for hard thresholding CV into gates.

patchIdeas.name: Reference calibrator
patchIdeas.patch: Use TLBX as a bench reference: 1V on OUT1 tunes a VCO to an octave, 5V on OUT2 acts as a trusted source for attenuverter/offset calibration.
steps: Leave MULT IN unpatched so the references are active.
Patch MULT OUT1 (1V) into your VCO's V/Oct input.
Play the VCO against a tuner and adjust the coarse knob until it sits exactly one octave above its 0V pitch.
Swap the cable to MULT OUT2 (5V) to verify five octaves — if it drifts, recalibrate TLBX's trimmer as shown in the manual.
patchIdeas.signalOut: MULT OUT1 — rock-steady 1V; MULT OUT2 — rock-steady 5V (DC reference).
patchIdeas.hear: Silent DC. You'll see the VCO jump up exactly one octave (then five octaves) with no drift over time.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:
VCO:
Output:

- TLBX (MULT OUT1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: 3x voltage multiplier (0-5V LFO becomes 0-15V)
patchIdeas.patch: Route a signal through MULT -> MIX -> DIFF and take A+B to triple the amplitude of a modulation source.
steps: Patch your LFO or envelope into MULT IN.
Leave MULT OUT1, MULT OUT2, MIX IN1, MIX IN2, MIX OUT, DIFF A and DIFF B all unpatched so every normalization cascades downstream.
Take DIFF A+B as the output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: DIFF A+B — the input signal multiplied by 3 (clipping at the +/-12V rails if you push too hard).
patchIdeas.hear: A slow LFO going 0-5V will now swing 0-15V (rail-limited). Great for dramatic filter sweeps from gentle modulators.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:
LFO:
VCF:

- LFO (Out) p> TLBX (MULT IN)
- TLBX (DIFF A+B) p> VCF (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: One-octave transposer
patchIdeas.patch: Add the 1V reference to a V/Oct line on DIFF A+B (up an octave) while DIFF A-B gives the same line transposed one octave down.
steps: Leave MULT IN unpatched so OUT1 reads 1V.
Patch MULT OUT1 (1V) into DIFF B.
Patch your sequencer's V/Oct into DIFF A.
Patch DIFF A+B to one VCO for the up-octave voice.
Patch DIFF A-B to a second VCO for the down-octave voice.
patchIdeas.signalOut: DIFF A+B — original pitch +1 octave; DIFF A-B — original pitch -1 octave.
patchIdeas.hear: A melody layered across three octaves with perfect tuning. Unpatch DIFF B to collapse both voices back to unison.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:
Sequencer:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
Mixer:

- Sequencer (V/Oct) p> TLBX (DIFF A)
- TLBX (MULT OUT1) p> TLBX (DIFF B)
- TLBX (DIFF A+B) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- TLBX (DIFF A-B) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- VCO 1 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bipolar to unipolar range converter
patchIdeas.patch: Turn a -5..+5V LFO into a clean 0-5V modulator for a unipolar destination by adding 5V and halving via MIX+DIFF.
steps: Leave MULT IN unpatched so OUT2 is 5V.
Patch MULT OUT2 (5V) into MIX IN1 — this adds 5V to the signal.
Patch your bipolar LFO into MIX IN2.
Leave MIX OUT unpatched so the sum cascades into DIFF.
Patch DIFF A-B (which receives MIX OUT halved) to your destination.
patchIdeas.signalOut: DIFF A-B — a clean 0-5V version of a -5..+5V input.
patchIdeas.hear: A bipolar LFO now sweeps a filter or wavefolder that only responds to positive CV without clipping the bottom half.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:
LFO:
VCF:

- TLBX (MULT OUT2) p> TLBX (MIX IN1)
- LFO (Out) p> TLBX (MIX IN2)
- TLBX (DIFF A-B) p> VCF (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: 5-channel mixer
patchIdeas.patch: Exploit every normalization to sum up to five CV or audio sources on DIFF A+B (with IN1 running at +6dB, the other four at unity).
steps: Patch sources 1 and 2 into MULT IN and MULT OUT1 is left open (signal cascades into MIX).
Patch sources into MULT IN, MIX IN1, MIX IN2, DIFF A and DIFF B — one per jack.
Leave MULT OUT1/OUT2 and MIX OUT unpatched so the cascade sums everything through to DIFF.
Take DIFF A+B as the master mix. MULT IN appears at roughly 2x, the other four at unity — attenuate source 1 upstream if you want unity gain on it too.
patchIdeas.signalOut: DIFF A+B — single mono sum of up to five inputs, DC-coupled so it works for audio or CV.
patchIdeas.hear: A full mini-mixer in 2HP. Pull any cable to that section's output to break its branch out of the mix.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:
Src 1:
Src 2:
Src 3:
Src 4:
Src 5:
Output:

- Src 1 (Out) -> TLBX (MULT IN)
- Src 2 (Out) -> TLBX (MIX IN1)
- Src 3 (Out) -> TLBX (MIX IN2)
- Src 4 (Out) -> TLBX (DIFF A)
- Src 5 (Out) -> TLBX (DIFF B)
- TLBX (DIFF A+B) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: CV comparator (zero-crossing gate)
patchIdeas.patch: Feed MIX OUT back into MIX IN2 to create a high-gain loop that slams to the rails, converting a bipolar CV into a hard +/-10V gate at each zero-crossing.
steps: Patch your bipolar CV source (LFO, VCO, slow envelope) into MIX IN1.
Use a short cable to patch MIX OUT back into MIX IN2.
Take MIX OUT (or its doubled version on DIFF A+B) to your gate destination.
patchIdeas.signalOut: MIX OUT — approximately +10V when the input is above 0V, -10V when below 0V.
patchIdeas.hear: A smooth LFO becomes a square-wave gate. Slow LFOs produce long trigger windows; audio-rate inputs produce a squared-up sub-octave.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:
LFO:
Envelope:

- LFO (Out) p> TLBX (MIX IN1)
- TLBX (MIX OUT) -> TLBX (MIX IN2)
- TLBX (MIX OUT) -> Envelope (Gate)
